title: Projects
text:
Featured Projects

Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
A public-facing example of how long-term wastewater treatment data can be assessed using reproducible reporting, descriptive statistics, reliability-based summaries, and engineering interpretation.
This page does not include site names, supplier names, proprietary information, confidential assessments, or project-specific data.
Topics include:

descriptive statistics
treatment performance
reliability assessment
statistical graphics
engineering discussion
reproducible technical reporting

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
section: Evidence-Based Wastewater Engineering and Applied Statistics
text:
Evidence-Based Wastewater Engineering and Applied Statistics
I develop and share public, non-confidential resources at the intersection of wastewater engineering, applied statistics, technology assessment, and reproducible technical reporting.
This site focuses on engineering statistics, wastewater treatment performance, reliability-based assessment, teaching, publications, and reproducible workflows using tools such as Quarto, R, Python, and GitHub.






NoteEducational and Professional Resource



This website is intended for educational and professional development purposes. It presents generalized engineering methods, statistical techniques, and reproducible workflows. Any examples are illustrative and do not represent specific facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Current Focus


Wastewater Technology Assessment
Reliability-based approaches for evaluating wastewater treatment performance using long-term field data, engineering interpretation, and probability-based metrics.
Technology framework


Engineering Statistics
Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including reliability analysis, uncertainty, visualization, and reproducible reporting.
Explore methods


Reports and Technical Notes
Selected public, non-confidential technical notes, research summaries, and reproducible examples related to wastewater and environmental engineering.
View reports


Teaching and Mentoring
Resources for students, engineers, and early-career professionals interested in applying statistics and reproducible workflows to engineering problems.
Teaching resources

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Guiding Principle

Engineering decisions are strongest when they are supported by transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
About
text:
🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.
Hello! I’m Vince Pileggi, a wastewater engineering expert, with the Ontario Ministry fo the Environment, Conservation and Parks and adjunct professor at the University of Guelph, School of Engineering.
Aside from my regular work I’m particularly passionate about the correct application of statistics to solving engineering problems when appropriate. I use VS Code [@VSCode], Python [@Python], GitHub [@GitHub], RStudio [@rstudio] for technical publishing and of course \(\LaTeX\) [@latex] for high-quality typesetting.
My dissertation was focused on municipal sludge treatment using an anaerobic membrane bioreactor and is available though my Research Gate profile [@pileggi_researchgate]. The thesis was written in \(LaTeX\) but included as significant amount of R-coding and statistics. It was a lot of fun with some frustration finishing it off 😄!
This project hosts statistical analyses and technical notes. Built with Quarto and published via GitHub Pages (Option A, /docs on main).

title: Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
text:
🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.








Treatment Performance Assessment Framework
This project presents a generalized framework for assessing the performance of wastewater treatment systems using engineering principles, applied statistics, and reproducible reporting.
The framework is intended for educational and professional development purposes and illustrates methods that can be applied to a wide range of treatment technologies and operating conditions.
Topics include:

defining performance objectives
selecting performance indicators
descriptive statistical analysis
trend analysis
treatment reliability
uncertainty evaluation
engineering interpretation
reproducible reporting using Quarto, Python, and Git
